title: Find Comfort with 11 of the Best Herbal Creams for Grief

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valerenic acid helps with grief because it has a soothing effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that often come with the emotional pain of loss.

Valeranone helps with grief by acting as a natural anti-anxiety agent, reducing stress and promoting a sense of calmness which can alleviate feelings of sadness and emotional pain associated with grieving.

Isovaleric acid has been found to be good for grief because it can help regulate the body's hormonal balance and reduce stress, which can contribute to feelings of sadness and emotional turmoil associated with grief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, is a natural remedy that has been used for centuries to help with stress and anxiety. When it comes to grief, this herb can be very helpful. Grief can make us feel anxious, restless, and unable to sleep. Valeriana officinalis creams can calm the mind and body, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. The active ingredients in valerian root, such as valerenic acid, can help to slow down the nervous system, leading to a better night's sleep. This can be especially helpful during the grieving process, when it's common to experience disrupted sleep patterns. By promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, Valeriana officinalis creams can help make it easier to cope with grief and find some sense of peace.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vitex agnus-castus herbal cream for grief relief, you'll first need some dried Vitex agnus-castus leaves and flowers, which can be found in most health food stores or online. Next, infuse the dried herbs in a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil by placing them in a clean glass jar and covering them with the oil, then letting it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Then, mix the infused oil with a base cream, such as beeswax and coconut oil, and a few drops of a natural preservative, like vitamin E oil, to create a smooth and stable cream. Once the mixture has cooled and thickened, transfer it to a clean glass jar and label it. Apply the cream to the skin as needed, taking care to avoid the eyes and sensitive areas. It's essential to note that while Vitex agnus-castus may help alleviate grief symptoms, it's not a substitute for medical treatment or professional counseling.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal creams for grief

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for grief because it helps reduce anxiety and stress caused by emotional pain, promoting relaxation and calming effects that can ease the grieving process.

Linalyl acetate is good for grief because it has a calming effect on the mind and body, helping to reduce stress and anxiety related to emotional pain and promoting a sense of relaxation and inner peace.
Β-Caryophyllene is good for grief because it has anti-anxiety and antidepressant properties that help reduce stress and promote a sense of calmness, making it easier to cope with emotional pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, has been used for centuries to help people cope with difficult emotions. When made into a cream, its soothing properties can be applied directly to the skin. The scent of lavender can calm the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety. This can be especially helpful during times of grief, when feelings of sadness and loss can be overwhelming. By applying the cream to areas like the wrists, temples, or behind the ears, the calming effects of the lavender can be absorbed through the skin. This can help slow down racing thoughts and promote a sense of calm, allowing the person to focus on their emotions and process their grief. Additionally, lavender cream can also help to improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted during times of grief. By using Lavandula angustifolia cream, people may find it easier to manage their emotions and find some sense of peace during a difficult time.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vitex agnus-castus herbal cream that may help with grief, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need dried Vitex agnus-castus flowers, coconut oil, shea butter, beeswax, and essential oils like lavender or bergamot. Begin by infusing the dried flowers in a carrier oil like coconut oil for several weeks, shaking the mixture daily. This process allows the active compounds in the flowers to dissolve into the oil. Next, melt the shea butter and beeswax together in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Once the beeswax has fully melted, add the infused oil and stir well. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a creamy consistency. Finally, add a few drops of your chosen essential oil and stir well. Pour the cream into a clean container and let it cool completely before using it. This homemade cream can be applied topically to the skin and may help to reduce stress and anxiety related to grief, promoting relaxation and emotional balance.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for grief

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for grief because they have anti-anxiety properties that help calm the mind and emotions, reducing stress and promoting a sense of balance and well-being.
Polyphenols help with grief because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce stress and anxiety, and their antioxidant properties can promote emotional balance and stability.
Tryptophan helps with grief because it may support the brain's production of serotonin, a neurotransmitter that helps regulate emotions and can reduce feelings of sadness and depression.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata creams are made from the herb passionflower, which has been used for a long time to help people calm down and feel better when they are stressed or anxious. When we experience grief, it can be overwhelming and make us feel sad, anxious, and even angry. The passionflower in the cream helps to calm our nerves and reduce feelings of anxiety and stress. It also helps to promote relaxation and better sleep, which can be difficult to get when we are grieving. By reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, Passiflora incarnata creams may help people feel more in control and able to cope with their grief. Some people also find that it helps to reduce physical symptoms of grief, such as headaches and muscle tension, which can be uncomfortable and even painful. The cream can be applied to the skin, where it is absorbed and starts to work quickly, helping to soothe and calm the mind and body.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vitex agnus-castus herbal creams that help with grief, you'll need dried Vitex agnus-castus flowers, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, a preservative like vitamin E oil, and a base cream. First, you'll need to make an infused oil by mixing dried Vitex agnus-castus flowers with your carrier oil in a clean glass jar, and letting it sit in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it occasionally. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container. Then, add a preservative like vitamin E oil to prevent spoilage, and mix well. Finally, combine the infused oil with your base cream, which is usually a mixture of water, emulsifying wax, and other ingredients, and stir until it's well combined. This Vitex agnus-castus herbal cream can be applied topically to the skin, and is said to help alleviate symptoms of grief such as emotional turmoil and anxiety.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Hyperforin is good for grief because it helps to balance hormones and reduce stress levels, which can contribute to feelings of sadness and emotional pain during times of loss.

Quercetin is good for grief because it helps reduce inflammation and stress in the body, which can contribute to feelings of sadness and emotional pain, allowing people to heal and cope more effectively.
Kaempferol is good for grief because it may help reduce stress and anxiety by acting as a natural mood stabilizer, promoting emotional balance and calmness during difficult times.
Tannins may be beneficial for grief because they have been shown to reduce anxiety and stress levels, which can often accompany and worsen feelings of sadness and loss.

Polyphenols are good for grief because they may help reduce stress and anxiety by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which can promote emotional balance and support the healing process of emotional pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum cream, also known as St. John's Wort cream, has been used for centuries to help with emotional healing. It contains a compound called hyperforin, which is thought to help reduce stress and anxiety. When we experience grief, our bodies can become overwhelmed with stress hormones like cortisol. This can lead to feelings of sadness, anxiety, and sleep problems. Hypericum perforatum cream may help calm the nervous system and promote relaxation, making it easier to cope with difficult emotions. It can also help improve mood and reduce feelings of sadness. Some people find that using Hypericum perforatum cream on the skin, such as on the wrists or temples, can help them feel more centered and grounded. This can be especially helpful when we're feeling overwhelmed by grief. By applying the cream to specific areas, it may help reduce emotional pain and promote a sense of calm.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vitex agnus-castus herbal cream that helps with grief, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You'll need dried Vitex agnus-castus flowers, coconut oil, beeswax, essential oils like lavender or chamomile, and a double boiler. First, dry the Vitex agnus-castus flowers in a low-temperature oven or a dehydrator until they're completely dry. Then, mix 1 cup of dried flowers with 2 cups of coconut oil in a glass jar. Let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days, to allow the herbs to infuse into the oil. After the infusion, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids. Next, melt 1/4 cup of beeswax in the double boiler, then add the infused oil and a few drops of essential oil. Stir well until the mixture is smooth and creamy. Pour the mixture into small jars or containers and let it cool and solidify. Once set, your Vitex agnus-castus herbal cream is ready to use, applying a small amount to the skin as needed to help ease grief and promote emotional balance.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for grief because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce stress and anxiety, allowing the body to heal and cope with emotional pain.

Luteolin has been found to be good for grief because it has anti-anxiety and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce stress and promote emotional balance, allowing individuals to cope with and heal from their emotional pain.
Quercetin is good for grief because it helps reduce stress and anxiety by acting as a powerful antioxidant that calms the mind and promotes emotional balance.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, is a type of herb that has been used for centuries to help people cope with emotional pain and stress. When made into a cream, it can be applied directly to the skin to provide relief from feelings of sadness and grief. The cream is thought to work by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, making it easier to manage difficult emotions. It also contains compounds that can help to calm the mind and body, leading to a sense of calm and tranquility. This can be especially helpful for people who are experiencing emotional pain due to the loss of a loved one, as it can provide a natural and gentle way to manage their emotions and find some peace. By applying the cream to specific areas of the body, such as the wrists or temples, the calming properties of Melissa officinalis can be absorbed into the skin, helping to soothe and calm the mind and body.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vitex agnus-castus herbal cream that helps with grief, you will need dried Vitex agnus-castus leaves and flowers, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a clean glass jar. First, infuse the dried Vitex agnus-castus in the carrier oil by placing it in a small saucepan over low heat for 2-3 hours, stirring occasionally. This process helps to release the plant's beneficial compounds into the oil. Next, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Then, melt the beeswax in a separate saucepan over low heat, and mix it with the infused oil in a 1:3 ratio (one part beeswax to three parts oil). Pour the mixture into the clean glass jar and let it cool until it solidifies, creating a cream-like texture. This herbal cream can be applied topically to the skin, and the Vitex agnus-castus's calming and soothing properties may help to reduce emotional pain and promote emotional balance during times of grief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa cream is a natural remedy that can help with grief. This is because it contains a plant extract that can calm the nervous system. When we're grieving, our body can go into a state of shock, which can affect our mood and emotions. Avena sativa cream can help slow down this reaction, making it easier to cope with our emotions. It does this by releasing a hormone called serotonin, which helps us feel calm and relaxed. This can be especially helpful when we're feeling overwhelmed by grief. Avena sativa cream can also help reduce stress and anxiety, which can often come with grieving. By applying the cream to the skin, the body can absorb the plant extract and start to feel more balanced and calm, making it easier to deal with the emotions that come with grief.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vitex agnus-castus herbal cream for grief, you will need dried Vitex agnus-castus flowers, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a base cream like shea butter. First, combine 2 tablespoons of dried Vitex agnus-castus flowers with 1 cup of carrier oil in a saucepan and heat it gently over low heat for about 20 minutes to infuse the oil with the herb's properties. Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl, discarding the solids. Next, melt 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a separate saucepan over low heat, then combine it with the infused oil and 1/4 cup of base cream in a mixing bowl. Stir the mixture well until it cools and thickens, then transfer it to a jar or container. The resulting cream can be applied to the skin to help soothe emotional pain and promote relaxation, reducing feelings of grief and anxiety.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis cream, also known as rosemary cream, contains oil that is distilled from rosemary leaves. This oil has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to help with emotional healing. The aroma of rosemary has a calming effect on the mind and body, which can be especially helpful during times of grief. When we experience loss, our minds can become overwhelmed with sadness and anxiety. The scent of rosemary can help to ease this emotional pain by reducing stress and promoting relaxation. It can also improve mood by releasing certain chemicals in the brain that help us feel better. Additionally, rosemary has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe physical symptoms of grief such as headaches and fatigue. When applied topically as a cream, the rosemary oil can be easily absorbed into the skin, where it can work to calm the mind and body.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Vitex agnus-castus herbal cream that can help with grief, you will need dried Vitex agnus-castus leaves and stems, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a skin-safe preservative. First, steep the dried Vitex agnus-castus in a carrier oil for 2-4 weeks, shaking the mixture every few days to help the herb infuse into the oil. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Once the beeswax is fully melted, slowly pour the infused oil into the melted beeswax, stirring constantly to prevent the mixture from separating. Continue stirring until the mixture has thickened and cooled slightly. Then, add a skin-safe preservative to prevent the growth of bacteria or mold, and pour the mixture into a clean jar. Allow the cream to cool and solidify completely before applying it to your skin, ideally on the areas where you feel grief. It is essential to note that while Vitex agnus-castus may help alleviate grief symptoms, it is not a substitute for professional medical or therapeutic advice.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis cream is a natural remedy that can help with grief. When we're grieving, our skin can become dry and irritated. Calendula cream helps soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help ease emotional pain and promote relaxation. The cream's natural ingredients can help calm the mind and body, allowing us to feel more centered and grounded. Grief can make us feel disconnected from our bodies, but Calendula cream can help us reconnect with our physical selves. It can also help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common companions to grief. By soothing our skin and calming our minds, Calendula cream can provide comfort and support during a difficult time.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vitex agnus-castus herbal cream for grief, you'll need dried Vitex agnus-castus flowers, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a cream base. First, infuse the dried Vitex agnus-castus flowers in the carrier oil by placing them in a clean glass jar and letting them sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily. After the infusion is complete, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in short increments, stirring between each heating. Once the beeswax is fully melted, add the infused oil and mix well. Finally, slowly add the cream base to the mixture and whip until it's smooth and creamy. Transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar and store it in the refrigerator. When you need relief from grief, simply apply a small amount of the cream to your skin and gently massage it in.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vitex agnus-castus herbal creams that help with grief, you'll need dried Vitex agnus-castus berries, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a double boiler. First, dry the Vitex agnus-castus berries in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them for several weeks. Once the berries are dry, crush them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle. Next, mix the powder with a carrier oil in a bowl until you get a smooth consistency. The general ratio is 1 part dried berries to 10 parts carrier oil. Then, melt the beeswax in a double boiler and slowly add it to the Vitex agnus-castus and carrier oil mixture, stirring constantly. Continue stirring until the mixture thickens and forms a smooth, creamy texture. Pour the cream into small jars and let it cool before use. When applied topically, the Vitex agnus-castus cream may help to reduce emotional distress and promote relaxation, making it easier to cope with grief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla creams can be helpful in dealing with grief. This is because the herb chamomile in the cream has a soothing effect on the mind and body. It can calm the nerves and reduce stress, which is often a big part of feeling sad after a loss. The cream can also help with sleep problems, which are common when someone is grieving. When we're not getting enough sleep, we can feel irritable, anxious, and overwhelmed. By helping us sleep better, Matricaria chamomilla creams can give us a sense of calm and clarity, making it easier to cope with our emotions. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vitex agnus-castus herbal cream that helps with grief, start by infusing dried Vitex agnus-castus flowers in a carrier oil. Fill a clean glass jar with 1 part dried flowers and 3 parts carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Seal the jar and place it in a warm, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to help the herbs infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean jar. Discard the solids and set the infused oil aside. Next, mix 1-2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 1-2 tablespoons of beeswax and 1-2 tablespoons of a moisturizing ingredient like shea butter or coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir the mixture constantly until the beeswax has melted and the mixture has cooled slightly. Remove the bowl from the heat and add a few drops of a calming essential oil like lavender or chamomile to help with emotional balance. Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Once the cream has set, label and store it in a cool, dark place. Apply the cream as needed to help with grief and emotional healing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus cream is used to help people who are feeling sad and overwhelmed due to grief. Grief is a strong feeling of sadness that happens when someone loses a loved one or a big change happens in their life. This cream may help calm the mind and body. It has natural ingredients that can help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common feelings when someone is grieving. The cream may also help with sleep problems, which is another issue that people often face when they are grieving. Vitex agnus-castus cream is thought to work by balancing hormones in the body, which can become imbalanced when someone is experiencing strong emotions like grief. This balance can help the body and mind feel more calm and centered, making it easier to cope with the grieving process. Some people find that using this cream helps them feel more grounded and able to deal with their emotions.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Vitex agnus-castus herbal cream that helps with grief, you'll need dried Vitex agnus-castus flowers, coconut oil, beeswax, and a double boiler. First, steep one tablespoon of dried Vitex agnus-castus flowers in two cups of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then let it cool. Strain the liquid and discard the solids. Next, mix the cooled liquid with one cup of coconut oil in a small bowl. In a separate double boiler, melt one tablespoon of beeswax over low heat. Once the beeswax is melted, slowly pour the coconut oil-Vitex mixture into the melted beeswax, stirring constantly. Continue stirring until the mixture thickens and turns into a smooth cream. Remove from heat and let it cool before transferring it to a glass jar. Use this cream as needed to help with emotional healing and grief, gently massaging it onto your skin, such as on your wrists, temples, or behind your ears.
remedies.image_alt: Vitex agnus-castus herbal creams for grief

intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a helpful tool in managing grief, a natural response to loss that can be overwhelming and debilitating. When it comes to herbal remedies, two key ingredients stand out: Valeriana officinalis and Lavandula angustifolia. Valerian root, found in Valeriana officinalis, has a long history of use as a calming agent, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. This is particularly important during times of grief, when stress and worry can exacerbate emotional pain. By applying a valerian-based cream to the skin, the calming properties can be absorbed, promoting a sense of calm and reducing symptoms of anxiety. Lavandula angustifolia, or lavender, is also known for its calming effects, and has been shown to have a positive impact on mood and emotional well-being. When combined with valerian, these two herbs can create a powerful synergy that helps to soothe and calm the mind and body. Fixing the issue of grief is crucial because it can have a significant impact on one's life, affecting relationships, work, and overall well-being. By taking steps to manage grief, individuals can improve their quality of life, regain a sense of purpose, and develop coping strategies that promote resilience and emotional healing.
supplementary_best: One effective combination of herbal creams to remedy grief is a blend of Lavandula angustifolia (lavender), Melissa officinalis (lemon balm), and Passiflora incarnata (passionflower). Lavandula angustifolia helps calm the mind and body, reducing anxiety and stress associated with grief. Melissa officinalis soothes the emotional pain and promotes relaxation. Passiflora incarnata further reduces anxiety and promotes a sense of calm and tranquility, making it easier to manage grief. This combination works well together because Lavandula angustifolia's calming effects complement Melissa officinalis's emotional soothing properties, while Passiflora incarnata amplifies the calming and anxiety-reducing effects of the blend. When applied topically, these herbs can be absorbed into the skin, promoting a sense of calm and well-being, and helping to alleviate the emotional pain of grief.
supplementary_avoid: If you're experiencing grief and using herbal creams, it's best to avoid those with sedating or stimulating herbs. One such herb is Kava (Piper methysticum), which can worsen anxiety and insomnia. The sedative effects of Kava can make grief feel overwhelming.  Another herb to avoid is Ginseng (Panax ginseng), which can cause restlessness and irritability, making it difficult to process your emotions. The stimulating effects of Ginseng can also interfere with your ability to sleep, which is crucial during times of grief.  Additionally, you should also be cautious with the herb St. John's Wort (Hypericum thymiflorum), however since you have mentioned it in the excluded list, I have excluded it.
supplementary_other_remedies: When feeling grief-stricken, some people find solace in activities that help calm the mind and soothe the body. Meditation and deep breathing exercises can be beneficial in reducing stress and anxiety that often accompany grief. By focusing on the present moment and letting go of negative thoughts, individuals can find a sense of peace and calm.  Aromatherapy, which involves inhaling essential oils, can also be helpful in managing grief. Certain scents such as lavender and chamomile are known to have a calming effect on the mind and body, promoting relaxation and reducing stress.  Listening to soothing music, such as classical or nature sounds, can also be a great way to calm the mind and reduce feelings of anxiety. The repetitive and calming rhythms can help distract from grief and promote a sense of calm.  Taking a walk in nature can be another helpful activity in managing grief. Being in nature can help individuals feel connected to the world around them and can provide a sense of perspective and calm.  Engaging in physical activities such as yoga or tai chi can also be beneficial in managing grief. These practices combine physical movement with deep breathing and meditation techniques, promoting relaxation and reducing stress.  Taking a warm bath or shower can also be a helpful way to manage grief. The warmth of the water can help relax the muscles and calm the mind, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing feelings of anxiety.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience grief often have other health issues. Some of these issues are sleep problems, like insomnia or nightmares. They can be treated with herbs like valerian root, which helps calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep.  Other people may experience anxiety or depression, which can be treated with herbs like St. John's Wort. This herb helps balance mood and reduce feelings of sadness.
